title: Lecturer Quiz: MLP Activation & Backpropagation - Multiple Response

questions.question: Why is the binary cross-entropy loss function generally preferred over the mean squared error (MSE) loss function for binary classification tasks? Select all that apply.

options.A: Gradient Behavior: Binary cross-entropy provides better gradient behavior, leading to faster convergence during training.
options.B: Interpretability of Probabilities: Binary cross-entropy directly interprets the output as probabilities, making it more intuitive for binary classification tasks.
options.C: Error Sensitivity: Mean squared error can be overly sensitive to outliers, which is less of a concern for binary cross-entropy in binary classification tasks.
options.D: Model Output Range: Binary cross-entropy ensures that the model outputs are strictly within the range of [0, 1].
options.E: Numerical Stability: Binary cross-entropy is more numerically stable, especially for extreme predictions close to 0 or 1.
